title: SNMP Device Polling from Edge
dataSources: `index=edge-hub-snmp sourcetype=edge_hub` — SNMP polls via Edge Hub to local devices
app: Splunk Edge Hub (SNMP integration), Splunk OT Intelligence
spl:
index=edge-hub-logs sourcetype=splunk_edge_hub_log "snmp" ("timeout" OR "unreachable")
| stats count by host, message
description: Edge Hub bridges OT/IT network segmentation by polling SNMP-enabled devices on isolated networks and forwarding data to Splunk Cloud.
value: Edge Hub bridges OT/IT network segmentation by polling SNMP-enabled devices on isolated networks and forwarding data to Splunk Cloud.
implementation: Configure SNMP polling via Edge Hub Advanced Settings → SNMP tab. Add devices by IP, set SNMP version (v1/v2c/v3), community string or v3 credentials, and define OIDs with aliases. Set polling interval (default 60s). Edge Hub polls local OT devices and forwards results to `edge-hub-snmp` index via HEC. This bridges the air-gap — enterprise Splunk never touches the OT network directly. Alert on device unreachability or metric threshold violations.
visualization: Table (device OID values), Status grid (device × poll status), Line chart (metric trends).
knownFalsePositives: Sensors offline during scheduled site visits, calibration, or replacement campaigns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (SNMP integration), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-snmp sourcetype=edge_hub` — SNMP polls via Edge Hub to local devices.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure SNMP polling via Edge Hub Advanced Settings → SNMP tab. Add devices by IP, set SNMP version (v1/v2c/v3), community string or v3 credentials, and define OIDs with aliases. Set polling interval (default 60s). Edge Hub polls local OT devices and forwards results to `edge-hub-snmp` index via HEC. This bridges the air-gap — enterprise Splunk never touches the OT network directly. Alert on device unreachability or metric threshold violations.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-logs sourcetype=splunk_edge_hub_log "snmp" ("timeout" OR "unreachable")
| stats count by host, message
```

Understanding this SPL

**SNMP Device Polling from Edge** — Edge Hub bridges OT/IT network segmentation by polling SNMP-enabled devices on isolated networks and forwarding data to Splunk Cloud.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-snmp sourcetype=edge_hub` — SNMP polls via Edge Hub to local devices. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (SNMP integration), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-logs; **sourcetype**: splunk_edge_hub_log. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-logs, sourcetype=splunk_edge_hub_log. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (device OID values), Status grid (device × poll status), Line chart (metric trends).
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with this area: snmp device polling from edge before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
